data.title: Ruby Receptionist: Never Miss Another Business Call
data.excerpt: Ruby Receptionist answers your calls and chats 24/7 with real agents and AI combined. See why thousands of small businesses choose Ruby to grow.

$media.alt: Ruby Receptionist: Never Miss Another Business Call

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through our links, TonyCiencia may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

I used to miss calls all the time. Not because I was lazy — because I was busy. I was on a client Zoom, elbow-deep in a project, or simply grabbing lunch. Every missed call felt like money walking out the door.
Then I found
Ruby Receptionist
, and it changed how I think about phone calls entirely.

If you run a small business, a law firm, a medical practice, a real estate agency, or any service-based company, this post is for you. Ruby is not just an answering service — it is a full-service virtual receptionist platform that combines
real human agents
with
AI technology
to answer every call and live chat on your behalf, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
Let me walk you through exactly why I recommend Ruby, what it does differently, and whether it is the right fit for your business.
Why Ruby Receptionist Is the Answer to Every Missed Call
Here is the uncomfortable truth:
85% of people who call a business and reach voicemail will not leave a message.
They hang up and call your competitor.

Ruby solves this problem completely. When someone calls your business number, a friendly, professional receptionist picks up — often within two rings. That receptionist is a real person trained on YOUR business, YOUR scripts, and YOUR preferences. They can:
Greet callers with your custom greeting
Answer common questions about your services
Transfer calls to you or your team
Take detailed messages and send them to you instantly
Schedule appointments through your calendar integration
Handle overflow when your in-house team is overwhelmed
And here is what sets Ruby apart from every other answering service I have tested:
the human-AI combination.

Ruby does not force you to choose between a robotic chatbot and an expensive live agent. It blends both seamlessly. The AI handles routine queries, qualifies leads, and manages chat interactions. The human agents handle the nuanced, high-stakes calls that require empathy and judgment.
The result? Your callers never feel like they are talking to a machine, and you never lose a lead to voicemail.
**Try Ruby Receptionist today and see the difference on your first day**
What Makes Ruby Stand Out From Other Virtual Receptionists
I have reviewed dozens of virtual receptionist and AI answering services. Most of them fall into one of two camps:
Pure AI solutions
— cheap, fast, but callers can tell they are talking to a bot. Not ideal for law firms, medical practices, or any business where trust matters.
Traditional answering services
— real people, but expensive, inconsistent quality, and limited hours.
Ruby sits in the sweet spot. It gives you
the warmth of a real receptionist
with
the efficiency and availability of AI.
Here are the things that genuinely impressed me:
Bilingual Support
Ruby offers English and Spanish receptionists. If your customer base includes Spanish speakers, this is a massive advantage that many competitors simply do not offer.
App & Dashboard

The Ruby mobile app lets you manage everything from your phone — check messages, listen to call recordings, update your availability, and see real-time call stats. It is clean, fast, and actually useful (not just a stripped-down mobile version of a desktop tool).
Customizable Call Handling

You decide exactly how Ruby handles your calls. Want all calls transferred to you? Done. Want calls screened and only urgent ones patched through? Easy. Want Ruby to handle everything and just send you a summary? That works too. You can change these rules by time of day, day of the week, or on the fly.
Live Chat Integration
Ruby is not just for phone calls. It also offers
live website chat

powered by the same human+AI hybrid model. A visitor lands on your site, clicks the chat widget, and gets an immediate, helpful response — whether they need directions to your office, want to know your pricing, or are ready to book an appointment.
If you want to go deeper into every feature, check out our guide on
what Ruby Receptionist is and how it works
New & Featured Capabilities You Should Know About
Ruby is not standing still. The platform has rolled out several features recently that make it even more powerful for growing businesses:
AI-Powered Call Summaries

After each call, Ruby now generates an AI-powered summary that captures the caller's name, reason for calling, any action items, and the outcome of the call. No more scrolling through long transcripts — you get the highlights instantly in your app or email.
Smarter Lead Qualification

Ruby's AI can now qualify leads during the intake process. It asks the right questions, collects key details, and flags high-value prospects so you can prioritize follow-up. For businesses that rely on inbound leads — think personal injury law, home services, or financial advisory — this is a game-changer.
Enhanced Integrations

Ruby now integrates more deeply with popular CRMs and scheduling tools. Whether you use Clio for legal practice management, Salesforce, or a simple Google Calendar setup, Ruby can push call data and appointments directly into your existing workflow. No double-entry, no manual transfers.
Improved Chat Analytics

The live chat feature now includes analytics dashboards that show you chat volume, response times, common questions, and conversion rates. This data helps you understand what your website visitors actually want — and adjust your messaging accordingly.
For a full breakdown of every update, visit our
Ruby new features roundup
**Explore Ruby's latest features with a free consultation**
Who Is Ruby Receptionist Ideal For?
Ruby is designed for
small businesses
— but not all small businesses. Here is who benefits the most:
Solo Practitioners & Small Teams

If you are a solo lawyer, accountant, therapist, or consultant, you cannot answer every call yourself. You are in meetings, in court, with clients. Ruby acts as your always-available front desk without the cost of hiring a full-time employee.
Law Firms

Ruby has deep roots in the legal industry. Their receptionists are trained in legal intake protocols, confidentiality requirements, and the urgency that legal calls demand. If you run a law firm and you are not using a service like Ruby, you are almost certainly losing cases to competitors who pick up the phone faster.
Medical & Dental Practices
Patient calls need to be handled with care. Ruby's receptionists can schedule appointments, answer insurance questions, and triage urgent calls — all while maintaining HIPAA-compliant practices.
Real Estate Agents
Showings, open houses, client calls — real estate professionals are constantly on the move. Ruby ensures that every lead call is answered professionally, even when you are showing a property.
Home Services Businesses

Plumbers, electricians, HVAC technicians — you are on job sites all day. Ruby captures every incoming call, qualifies the lead, and either books the job or sends you a message so you can call back when you are off the ladder.
E-Commerce & Online Businesses
With Ruby's live chat feature, even online businesses can benefit. Visitors get instant answers instead of waiting hours for an email reply, which directly increases conversion rates.
For more detailed scenarios, check out our
Ruby use cases guide
How Ruby Works: A Quick Walkthrough
Getting started with Ruby is refreshingly simple. Here is the typical onboarding flow:
Sign up and choose your plan.
Ruby offers several tiers based on the number of minutes you expect to use. Plans are flexible — you can scale up or down as your needs change.
Set up your greeting and call handling rules.
Ruby's team helps you craft a custom greeting and configure exactly how you want calls handled. This takes about 15–20 minutes.
Forward your business line to Ruby.
You can forward your existing number or get a new one from Ruby. Either way, setup is instant.
Download the app.
The Ruby app becomes your command center. Check messages, manage settings, and stay connected from anywhere.
Start receiving calls.
From day one, Ruby answers your calls, takes messages, transfers calls, and sends you real-time notifications.
The entire process can be up and running in less than a business day. No complicated phone system installation. No IT department required.
Want a detailed step-by-step? Our
how to use Ruby tutorial
covers every screen and setting.
Ruby Receptionist Pricing: Flexible Plans That Grow With You
One of the things I appreciate most about Ruby is its
transparent, scalable pricing.
You are not locked into a massive contract. You choose a plan based on the minutes you need, and you can adjust at any time.
Ruby's plans typically include:
A set number of live receptionist minutes per month
24/7 availability
(yes, nights, weekends, and holidays)
Live chat support
on your website
Spam call filtering
(this alone saves hours of wasted time)
Mobile app access
with call recordings and messages
Bilingual support
(English and Spanish)

The pricing is designed so that even a solo business owner can afford professional phone answering. And when you compare it to the cost of hiring even a part-time receptionist — salary, benefits, training, downtime — Ruby pays for itself quickly.
For our full pricing breakdown and tips on choosing the right plan, read our
Ruby tips & best practices article
**Get your personalized Ruby quote and start answering every call**
My Honest Take: Why I Keep Recommending Ruby
I have been in the software review space for years. I test tools constantly. Some are good, some are forgettable. Ruby is one of the rare tools I recommend with genuine enthusiasm.
Here is why:
It solves a real, expensive problem.
Every missed call is a missed opportunity. Ruby eliminates that problem entirely.
The quality is consistent.
I have listened to dozens of Ruby-handled calls. The receptionists are professional, warm, and accurate. They do not sound like they are reading a script — they sound like they work for YOUR company.
The technology keeps improving.
Ruby is not resting on its laurels. The AI features, the integrations, the analytics — they keep adding value month after month.
The pricing is honest.
No hidden fees. No surprise charges. You know what you are paying and what you are getting.
The support is excellent.
Ruby's own customer service team is responsive and helpful. There is something poetic about a customer service company that has great customer service.

If you are on the fence, I encourage you to just try it. Most businesses see the impact within the first week — fewer missed calls, more booked appointments, and a more professional impression on every caller.
Frequently Asked Questions
How is Ruby different from a traditional answering service?

Traditional answering services use scripted agents who may or may not know your business. Ruby combines trained human receptionists with AI technology to deliver personalized, professional call handling 24/7. The result is higher quality interactions and better lead capture than most legacy services.
Can Ruby handle calls in Spanish?
Yes. Ruby offers bilingual receptionists who can handle calls in both English and Spanish. This is a significant advantage for businesses serving diverse communities.
Does Ruby integrate with my CRM or scheduling tool?

Ruby integrates with a growing number of popular tools including Clio, Salesforce, Google Calendar, and more. Their team can help you set up integrations during onboarding so call data flows directly into your existing workflow.

children.text: How much does Ruby Receptionist cost per month?

Ruby offers several pricing tiers based on the number of live receptionist minutes you need each month. Plans are flexible and scalable, so solo business owners and growing teams alike can find a plan that fits their budget. You can request a personalized quote directly through their website.
Can Ruby Receptionist answer calls outside business hours?
Yes. Ruby provides 24/7 call answering, including nights, weekends, and holidays. Your callers will always reach a real, trained receptionist — never a voicemail box.
Is Ruby Receptionist good for law firms?

Absolutely. Ruby has deep experience serving legal practices. Their receptionists are trained in legal intake protocols, confidentiality standards, and the urgency that legal calls require. Many law firms use Ruby to capture leads and ensure no potential client call goes unanswered.
